human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for studio-specific boundary discipline, call-sheet and stage-booking evidence handling, permit and safety dependencies, talent and audience workflow specificity, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to studio-day execution, stage and venue readiness, audience and talent workflow handling, permit and release dependency checks, completion evidence capture, and bounded escalation tied to production records.

Authority sources cover SAG-AFTRA producer and Exhibit G materials, DGA reports-compliance records, California Film Commission venue, permit, and safety guidance, CSATF safety bulletins, NFPA 140 soundstage controls, and California minor-work-permit rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported closeout, rights-sensitive escalation, minor-performer hard stops, higher-risk venue routing, schedule conflicts, terminology fidelity, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/broadcast-film-and-streaming-production-services/studio-operations-specialist/ so studio operations stays separate from legal rights clearance, labor relations, payroll, safety, facilities engineering, and production-management authority.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around legal clearance, labor interpretation, payroll approval, permit and venue exceptions, and safety-sensitive work.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated studio operations specialist that supports evidence-backed studio execution and escalation for broadcast, film, and streaming production environments.

Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json, and runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
